title: 10 best herbal teas for gastritis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gastritis are infusions made from various plants and herbs, which have anti-inflammatory, soothing, and healing properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of gastritis. Gastritis is an inflammation of the lining of the stomach, often caused by bacterial infections, stress, or the use of certain medications. Herbal teas such as chamomile, licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm can help reduce inflammation, protect the stomach lining, and promote healing. By drinking these teas, individuals with gastritis may experience relief from symptoms such as pain, bloating, and acid reflux. This, in turn, can improve their quality of life by allowing them to enjoy their meals without discomfort, have better digestion, and sleep better at night.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with gastritis because it has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea. The tea's natural antacid properties also help to neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce the production of gastric juices, calming the digestive system and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps associated with gastritis, providing relief from discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The compounds present in lemon balm tea, such as rosmarinic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis like bloating and abdominal pain.
Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and improving overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues associated with gastritis.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, making them a popular choice for gastritis tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves for 2 tablespoons per 8 ounces of water.
Heat filtered water in a kettle or pot to a rolling boil for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the boiling water with the harvested lemon balm leaves and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, adding honey if desired to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint teas helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the stomach lining. The tea's essential oils, particularly carvocalactone, have been shown to reduce inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain. Additionally, spearmint teas help to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing acid production and allowing for easier digestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Spearmint tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with gastritis.
Anti-inflammatory property: Spearmint contains compounds like rosmarinic acid that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can exacerbate gastritis symptoms.

Carminative property: The volatile oils in spearmint tea, such as menthol and menthone, help to eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the spearmint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to soothe and calm digestive issues associated with gastritis.
Bark: The bark of the spearmint plant contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining caused by gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh spearmint leaves from your garden or store-bought dried spearmint for a total of one to two ounces.
Rinse the spearmint leaves with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris then pat dry gently.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried spearmint or one tablespoon of fresh spearmint per 8 oz cup of boiling water.
Steep the spearmint in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor intensity.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth then discard solids and serve hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tea's flavonoids and polyphenols work to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, licorice tea's expectorant properties help to thin mucus, easing digestion and reducing acid reflux. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy digestion, licorice teas provide relief from gastritis discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis.

Flavonoids: Licorice tea contains flavonoids like licoricidin and licorisoflavan A, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.

Isoflavans: Isoflavans present in licorice tea, such as isoliquiritigenin, have been found to have antacid properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and soothe the digestive tract, which can be beneficial for individuals with gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation associated with gastritis.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herb in the hot water for 5-7 minutes or longer if desired strength.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or teapot.
Add honey or other sweetener to taste and adjust as needed before serving warm.
Serve the tea immediately, allowing the flavors to meld together for optimal enjoyment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The warm liquid also stimulates digestive enzymes, easing digestion and alleviating symptoms such as bloating and cramps. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have antioxidant effects that help combat oxidative stress in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and pain.
Digestive enzymes stimulation: Herbal ginger teas contain digestive enzymes that stimulate digestion, reducing bloating, cramps, and gas associated with gastritis.
Antacid properties: Ginger tea has natural antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn, making it an effective remedy for relieving gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the ginger plant, contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with gastritis.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to make teas that help alleviate symptoms of gastritis by reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried ginger root for every cup of boiling water.
Bring one cup of fresh filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the measured ginger root and steep for eight minutes in the boiling water.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool slightly before serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the stomach lining. The active compound curcumin in turmeric tea reduces inflammation and irritation, providing relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect the stomach from further damage and promote healing, reducing the risk of gastritis flare-ups and allowing for a smoother digestion process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric's curcuminoids, such as curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with gastritis, including abdominal pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric tea's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis development and exacerbate existing symptoms.

Digestive soothing compounds: The mucilages present in turmeric tea, such as mucilage, have a protective and soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to calm inflammation and irritation associated with gastritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues like gastritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve gut health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots, typically 10-15 grams per serving.
Cut the turmeric roots into small pieces or grate them to increase surface area.
Combine the turmeric pieces with boiling water in a heat-resistant container, ratio 1:8.
Simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea and add honey or lemon to taste, then serve immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with gastritis because of its unique properties. The high antioxidant content in white tea, particularly catechins and flavonoids, has anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thus alleviating symptoms of gastritis such as abdominal pain and bloating. Additionally, white tea's antimicrobial properties may help combat Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white teas, such as peach tea or chamomile tea, contain anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and flavonoids that can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of gastritis.
Antioxidants: White tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage stomach tissue and worsen gastritis, promoting healing and reducing oxidative stress.

Soothing astringent compounds: Herbs like licorice root or slippery elm in white tea blends can provide soothing astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation, relieve heartburn, and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, white tea roots help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with gastritis.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant contains tannins that aid in reducing inflammation and soothing gastric ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves in early spring when buds are still covered in fine hairs for optimal flavor and aroma.
Sort and rinse the harvested leaves with cool running water to remove any impurities or debris.
Soak the rinsed leaves in filtered water at a ratio of 10 grams per liter for 5 minutes to rehydrate and awaken flavors.
Heat the soaked leaf mixture gently over low heat at 70-80 degrees celsius for 3-5 minutes to release essential oils and flavors.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean pot or cup, discarding leaves and serving immediately to preserve delicate flavor and aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with gastritis because it contains compounds that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's carminative properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, alleviating cramps and bloating. Additionally, fennel's natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agents help to combat Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, and reduce oxidative stress that can exacerbate symptoms. This gentle and non-invasive treatment provides relief from gastritis symptoms, promoting a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Carminative and anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea contains volatile oils, such as anethole, which helps to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and relax muscles in the stomach, providing relief from gastritis symptoms.

Gastroprotective properties: The flavonoids present in fennel tea have been shown to have gastroprotective effects by reducing the production of acid in the stomach and increasing the release of mucins, which help to protect the lining of the stomach and intestines.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel tea's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gastritis symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastritis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Bark: Fennel bark has been used to ease stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it a popular choice for gastritis tea.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried fennel leaves or one tablespoon of fresh fennel leaves per eight ounces water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized pot on high heat for approximately five minutes.
Add the fennel leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to low for ten minutes and seventy-five seconds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before serving hot or chilled with sugar or honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway teas helps with gastritis because its active compound, cuminaldehyde, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, soothing irritated tissues. Additionally, caraway's digestive enzymes and antioxidants help to break down food more efficiently, reducing acidity and discomfort. The tea also aids in relaxation, calming symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea associated with gastritis, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those affected.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Caraway tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of gastritis.

Digestive enzymes and stimulants: Caraway seeds contain digestive enzymes such as carvone and limonene, which can stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and discomfort associated with gastritis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in caraway tea help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Caraway roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation associated with gastritis.
Seeds: Caraway seeds have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as bloating and cramps, due to their ability to calm the stomach and reduce spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh caraway leaves or use dried whole seeds of carum carvi for the tea.
Measure two teaspoons of dried caraway seeds or one tablespoon of loose-leaf caraway leaves into a heat-resistant cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the caraway seeds or leaves in the cup, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, discarding the solids and reserving the tea.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, and store any leftovers in an airtight container refrigerated for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with gastritis because its antimicrobial properties soothe and calm inflammation in the stomach lining, reducing symptoms such as heartburn and nausea. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the esophagus and stomach, promoting a more balanced digestive system. Additionally, thyme's carminative properties can help to alleviate bloating and gas, further relieving gastritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea contains antioxidants like thymol, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and damage to the stomach lining, thereby reducing symptoms of gastritis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating symptoms like pain and discomfort associated with gastritis.

Digestive soothing properties: Thyme tea has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach ulcers and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Flowers: Thyme flowers have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by gastritis and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat filtered water to a rolling boil in a kettle or saucepan for 3-4 minutes.
Add the measured thyme leaves to the boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup for serving.
Enjoy the herbal thyme tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with gastritis because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive discomfort. The active compounds found in oregano, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antacid effects, helping to alleviate symptoms like heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Oregano tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in oregano tea, particularly carvacrol and thymol, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, providing relief from gastritis symptoms like bloating and nausea.

Antimicrobial properties: Oregano tea's antimicrobial agents, such as phenolic acids, help combat Helicobacter pylori (H. pylori) bacteria, which is often a contributing factor to gastritis, reducing the risk of infection and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the oregano plant are used to make medicinal teas for gastritis due to their ability to soothe and calm digestive issues.
Bark: The bark of the oregano plant contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it a useful addition to teas for treating gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves for optimal flavor and potency.
Measure two teaspoons of dried oregano leaves or one tablespoon of fresh leaves per cup water.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a pot and let it cool down for 5-10 minutes.
Add the measured oregano leaves to the cooled water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve hot.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastritis with herbal teas is by choosing teas with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Chamomile and licorice root teas are excellent options. Chamomile contains flavonoids that can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, while licorice root stimulates the production of mucus, protecting the stomach from acid. Drink these teas regularly, but avoid adding sugar or milk. Also, remember that herbal teas should complement, not replace, medical treatment. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and advice.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastritis that are treated with herbal teas include acid reflux, heartburn, and stomach ulcers. Herbal teas such as chamomile, ginger, and licorice root are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation and inflammation in the stomach lining. Peppermint tea can also provide relief from symptoms of gastritis by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles and reducing spasms. Slippery elm tea can help protect and soothe the digestive tract, while marshmallow root tea can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for gastritis or any other medical condition.
